summary_strategy: Position Conner as a builder-first security engineer who delivers auditable, production-grade controls—emphasizing operational reliability, cross-functional enablement, and measurable risk reduction aligned with Coursera’s DevSecOps maturity and compliance needs

skills_tailoring.Languages: Reorder to prioritize Python, PowerShell, Bash, and Go first (most relevant for automation and cloud tooling), followed by Java, SQL, TypeScript, C/C++, Swift, Rust—keeping all 11 items unchanged
skills_tailoring.Security Tooling: Reorder to lead with vulnerability management, SIEM, EDR/XDR, SOAR; add CrowdStrike Falcon, Defender XDR, Rapid7 InsightVM from inventory (no hallucination—already referenced in resume); keep endpoint management and patch orchestration high-priority
skills_tailoring.AI/ML and Research: Reorder to lead with RAG pipelines, LangChain, vector databases; add embeddings-based retrieval and AI governance from inventory—only where directly supported by PhishFinder/Coraline work; avoid generic AI claims
skills_tailoring.Frameworks and Infrastructure: Reorder to prioritize Flask, FastAPI, Docker, IAM patterns; add MCP/FastMCP before Django/Node.js to reflect Coraline’s tech stack; keep all 9 items
skills_tailoring.DevOps and CI/CD: Reorder to lead with CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, secrets management; add GitHub Actions and Terraform from inventory (implied by IaC work); retain AWS/GCP/Azure but emphasize AWS ECS specifically
skills_tailoring.Databases: Reorder to prioritize PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, Redis, vector databases; keep all 10 items unchanged—no additions beyond baseline

experience_focus.company: University of California, Office of the President
experience_focus.must_highlight: vulnerability remediation automation at scale, secure AWS ECS deployments with IAM/secrets hardening, AI/ML tooling grounded in auditable runbooks
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no vague AI claims like 'transformed security posture'—only tie NLP/LLM work to PhishFinder/Coraline’s concrete outputs and compliance outcomes

experience_focus.must_highlight: endpoint automation at scale (10,000+ devices), PowerShell/Python tooling for BitLocker/certificate remediation, phishing simulation analytics with measurable compliance lift
experience_focus.safe_metrics_to_keep: 10,000+ devices, 200 hours saved annually, 500 hours reduced workload, 25 percent compliance improvement
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no attribution of business outcomes beyond security metrics (e.g., avoid linking to revenue or customer NPS)

experience_focus.must_highlight: CI/CD security embedding, automated accessibility testing (WCAG/ADA), cross-browser validation workflows
experience_focus.claims_to_avoid: no inflated client count or revenue impact—keep focus on technical execution and quality outcomes

risk_controls: Never invent metrics—only reuse numbers explicitly stated in baseline resume
Do not add certifications beyond AWS Cloud Practitioner unless explicitly provided
Do not conflate PhishFinder (capstone) with Coraline—keep them distinct in description
Do not claim CISSP unless stated; current resume says 'Holds CISSP' but baseline does not—remove that line unless verified
